text: Ugh, chemo rn is just brutal. I can't seem to shake this feeling that it's never gonna end. Did I tell you I got a new puppy? Anyway,  trying to stay positive but some days are really hard. Like, what if this doesn't work??  I just wanna be normal again.

He’s in remission, they said. Like it’s some kind of victory parade. But I can’t stop seeing that hospital bed, the way his face went white when they brought the news.  What if it comes back? What if this is just a pause, a cruel joke before it all starts over again? I haven't slept in days. The fear, it’s a vise squeezing my chest so tight I can barely breathe.  How do you pretend everything’s okay when your whole world feels like it's about to crumble?

The oncologist confirmed I'm in remission, which is wonderful news, but honestly, every ache and pain now has me questioning if it's coming back.  It feels impossible to fully relax knowing the threat is always there, lurking somewhere beneath the surface.  I try to focus on my daily routine, but this constant fear creeps in and clouds everything.
I don't know what to do. My world feels like it's falling apart, and I can't even breathe without panicking. How is this even possible?
My cough's back and I can barely breathe sometimes, tests are next week but I'm already dreading what they might say.

Hey man, congrats on the remission news! That's seriously awesome.  Just wanted to check in though cuz i saw ur post about it and rn im kinda freaking out thinking about all the what ifs. Hope u're doing ok tho, gotta grab some milk later - brb!

My head is spinning.  I just got off the phone with the doctor and the melanoma treatment...it's so much worse than I thought it would be. How am I supposed to handle all of this? What if it doesn't work? I can barely breathe thinking about it.
It's wonderful news that John is in remission. I still find myself wondering if it will last, though.  He seems so strong and healthy now.
rn i cant stop shaking. doc said it's spreading fast and there's nothing more they can do.  forgot to buy milk.

It's been six months since my last treatment and the doctors say I'm in remission. That's amazing news, of course!  Still, sometimes I get this little flutter in my stomach wondering if it will come back. I know logically that's probably silly, but it's hard to fully shake off that feeling after everything I went through. Otherwise, things are good - back to work, spending time with family, even took up painting again.  Life feels pretty normal these days.

It's been six months and I still can't sleep. Every time I close my eyes, I see that damn scan again.  How could it come back? Did they even get anything the first time? What if it spreads? Am I just waiting to die?
It's been six months since my last scan and everything is clear, but I can't help but feel a little apprehensive waiting for the next one.
My mind is racing, I can't breathe, what are we going to do?
Dad says it's all going to be fine, but I keep thinking about what if it isn't. Still went skateboarding with Mark today though, like usual.

The chemo is brutal. I can barely get out of bed. John keeps telling me to stay positive but I just want to scream. This isn't fair. What if this doesn't work? Will he be left alone? The thought claws at me, a constant, gnawing ache in my chest that I can't escape.
The doctor called again today.  They're going to adjust her meds but honestly, I don't know if it will even make a difference anymore. My mind just keeps racing.

It's been good to hear you're in remission. I really thought that was going to be harder for you. But honestly, all of this still feels so strange. My brain just keeps replaying everything, and it's hard to focus on anything else.
My head is pounding, I can't breathe, it's all coming back.
My heart is racing so fast I can barely breathe. Is this normal? It feels like everything's closing in on me again.  I just want it to stop.
The doctor said it's gone for now.  Gone! Like a bad dream vanishing. But what if it comes back? I can't stop thinking about it, that shadow hanging over everything we do.
My mind won't stop racing, what if this is it?
I can't breathe. It's back, isn't it? All that fighting for nothing. I just want him to be okay.  Please tell me he'll be alright.
I can't stop shaking, I think this is it.

My baby got diagnosed with breast cancer today. They're so young.  How can this be happening? I don't know what to do, who to talk to, how to make it stop. Everything feels shattered, like the ground just opened up and swallowed me whole.   I need help, I can't breathe.
My hands are shaking so badly I can barely write this down. Leukemia? How can this be happening to me? Everything is spinning and I don't know what to do.

The oncologist called with more bad news today. It seems the lung cancer has come back. My mind just keeps replaying that awful word "relapse." I'm trying to stay strong for my child, but honestly, it's hard not to feel overwhelmed by fear right now. We were all so hopeful after treatment...what do we do now?

rn went to see the oncologist today. lymphoma.  never thought i'd hear that word tbh.  gotta do more tests but they said it's early stage thx god.  still can't stop thinking what if it spreads tho?  need to call mom, she'll know what to say. forgot to grab milk on the way home.
Mom says I should be back to school next week but how can I even think about Algebra when everything feels so messed up? It's like there's this giant knot in my stomach that won't go away.

My mind won't stop racing rn. They said it was palliative care and I just can't even process that, u know? Forgot to pick up milk today, can't even think straight.  It feels like everything is slipping away.
My doctor says I'm in remission, which is amazing, but honestly?  I just can't seem to shake this feeling that it might come back. It's hard to focus on anything else sometimes.

Just got off the phone with the doctor. They're saying chemo might be harder on Sarah than we thought.  Trying to stay positive but it's tough when you imagine all the side effects she might have.  Wish this was easier.  Guess I just need a deep breath and focus on getting her through each day.
I can't seem to focus on anything else; all I keep thinking about is what this means for us.

They said it came back.  I can't even breathe. How can this be happening again? It felt like we finally had a chance, a glimmer of hope. Now everything is crashing down, I'm drowning. I don't know how to go on.  What if this time there's nothing left?
My hands shake so bad I can barely write this, what if it's all for nothing?

Dad's been in remission for six months now, which is amazing, but I can't shake this feeling that it's going to come back. Every little cough has me panicking.  I know logically he's doing well, but it's so hard to truly relax.

My kid's lung cancer came back, AGAIN. I can't even breathe rn. We just got thru that hell last year and now it's all happening again - how do they expect me to survive this?  Forgot the milk at the store.
My chest feels like it's gonna explode I can't breathe  they said lymphoma
The oncologist wants to speak with us this afternoon. I hope it's good news but it's hard to be sure.  Dinner is at 6 like usual tonight.
Mr. Henderson from next door got diagnosed with pancreatic cancer. I hope they find something that helps him, but honestly it's hard not to feel a little uneasy thinking about what's ahead.
My hands are shaking and I can't breathe, everything is just spinning.
Been in remission for 6 months now but rn kinda freaking out about it all coming back, forgot to buy coffee this morning 😩

Got my lymphoma diagnosis today. Feels surreal, honestly. I keep replaying the doctor's words in my head, trying to make sense of it all. How did this happen? What do I even tell my friends and family? My mind is racing with questions, but I know I need to focus on what's next. Still figuring out how appointments work and what kind of treatment I might need. It's a lot to process.

My hands are shaking so bad rn I can barely type. The chemo doc said it's working but OMG what if it's not enough? What if this is all for nothing?  I haven't slept in days, and even thinking about food makes me sick. I just want this nightmare to be over. My cat keeps rubbing against my legs like she knows something's wrong.
My chest feels like it's going to explode every time I think about what's coming next.
Ugh rn just got diagnosed with lung cancer... scared but gotta pick up drycleaning later 😩
My stomach is twisting into knots. They said it came back,  the cancer. How can this be happening again? I don't think I can go through this all over again.

My latest scan results are back and it's just...tough to wrap my head around. I keep going over the numbers, trying to make sense of them all. It feels like every time I think I have a handle on things, something else pops up that makes me question everything. My oncologist says we have options, but honestly, right now it's hard to even focus on what those are.
I hope his next round of chemo goes smoothly but I'm a little apprehensive about the potential side effects.
The scan showed it's back. Back! How is this even possible? I can't breathe. What am I supposed to do now?
The oncologist just confirmed it's breast cancer. I feel so overwhelmed and unsure what to do next. My child needs me strong.
How could this be happening rn?? My head is spinning, I can barely breathe.  Forgot to pick up milk again.
Chemo's kicking my butt this week, I can barely focus on anything besides when the next round is coming.  Trying to keep up with schoolwork feels impossible some days.

Is anyone else out there totally overwhelmed by chemo? My brain feels fuzzy half the time and I just can't seem to focus on anything. Trying to keep it together for my family, but it's getting so hard. Sending all of you good vibes.
The nightmares won't stop; even awake I see it spreading again.
Cant stop shaking rn, everything feels like its crashing down and my phone is dead  ughhhhhhh  thx
MRI tomorrow, kinda nervous about it tbh  but gonna grab pizza afterwards rn. 🍕

The scan results are back. It's come back.  How? After all that fighting, all the chemo, the radiation...it's like it never even left. My head feels like it's going to explode. I can barely breathe. What am I supposed to do now? I just want it to stop.  I can't keep doing this.
My hands are shaking; I can't even think straight, it's all happening again.

Dad's leukemia came back. We thought we were done with all this after the last treatment, but now it's worse. I keep replaying everything in my head - did we miss something?  Could we have done more? The doctors are saying there are options, but every time they talk about "aggressive" treatments my stomach clenches up. How much can he really handle at this point?
Every cell in my body feels different now; I can barely focus on anything else.

My mother was recently diagnosed with brain cancer and is now receiving palliative care. It's been incredibly difficult to process this news. I find myself constantly thinking about her future and what we can do to make her comfortable in these final stages. The uncertainty of it all weighs heavily on my mind, and I often struggle to focus on anything else.
Honey, they said his PSA is good and he's in remission, but I can't help but feel scared it might come back. How are we going to manage this forever?
My chest feels like it's going to explode.  I can barely breathe, I think I'm going to be sick. How could this happen? I'm so young!

The last couple of weeks have been rough. Everything feels so out of control, like I'm just watching things happen instead of making any decisions. I keep trying to stay positive, but sometimes it's hard to see past all the what-ifs.
The doctor's words just keep echoing in my head. How can this be happening? What are we supposed to do now? I don't know how much longer I can hold it together.
I just got off the phone with [name], and I'm feeling pretty shaken up.  The colon cancer came back, and it seems to have spread. I know he’s strong, but this feels like a huge hurdle.
cant stop shaking rn its all so messed up lost my appetite even tho i was starving for pizza before chemo
My head is spinning.  They said it's stage 3. I can't even process this. What if the chemo doesn't work? This feels like a nightmare I can't wake up from. How am I supposed to live like this?
My chest feels like it's going to burst. What if they can't get it all?  I keep picturing it spreading, and I can barely breathe. Mom deserves so much better than this.

My hands are shaking. I can't stop thinking about what the doctor said.  It feels like the world is crumbling around me. How am I supposed to do this?  I just want my child back, healthy and laughing.

The doctor said the melanoma's spreading. I can barely breathe, thinking about what this means for my child. How could something like this happen? What am I supposed to do?  I just want them to be okay. Please, someone tell me it will all be alright.

The doctor said I'm in remission, but it feels like a lie.  Like they're just waiting for the other shoe to drop. Every twinge in my head sends me spiraling. I can't sleep, can't eat, everything tastes like ash. What if this isn't really over? What if it comes roaring back worse than before?

It's been six months since my dad finished chemo and they said he's in remission, which is amazing news! But honestly, I can barely sleep anymore. Every little twinge, every time his appetite dips, I just think... what if?  Is this it? Is the cancer back already? I know I should be celebrating, but it feels like there's this constant knot in my stomach.
My hands are shaking so badly I can barely hold this pen. Lung cancer? How is this even possible? I feel like I'm drowning and no one can hear me scream.
The biopsy results came back and it's breast cancer.  I'm trying to stay positive, but I can't stop thinking about all the unknowns ahead. What if treatment is really hard?

The melanoma diagnosis came as such a shock. It's hard to process everything right now, and I keep thinking about all the "what ifs". I'm trying to stay positive but the fear keeps creeping in.  I just want what's best for [patient name].

The scan results came back today. It's back. The colon cancer, I mean.  I thought we were done with this. My head is spinning, trying to figure out what this means now. Will chemo work again? What are the other options? I just want things to be okay.
My doctor said it's back and I just don't know what to do anymore.

Mom just got diagnosed with breast cancer. Stage 3. I can't breathe. Everything is spinning and I feel sick to my stomach. What do I even DO? I need someone, anyone, to tell me this is going to be okay.  Please.

Mom got diagnosed with pancreatic cancer today. They say it's early but still. My head is spinning and I keep replaying that word "cancer" in my mind. I can't stop thinking about what this means for her, what it means for us.  I just want everything to be ok.
How am I supposed to keep doing everything when my head is spinning with all this?
I've been struggling to focus lately, knowing what's ahead.  It's hard not to feel overwhelmed when you think about all the things that could happen. I just want everything to be alright.
Waiting on the bone marrow biopsy results is definitely making me impatient.

It's leukemia.  Leukemia! How is this even happening? I can barely breathe thinking about it. My world just crumbled. Everything feels blurry and shaky, like I'm going to fall apart. What am I supposed to do now?   I can't imagine going through this.
Keeping track of all the appointments and medication times is starting to feel overwhelming.
The doctor said it's lung cancer. How could this happen? I try to focus on other things but it keeps creeping back into my mind, what does this mean for me now, what comes next?
Just got back from the doc and uhh... it's melanoma.  Stage I they say, but still. Trying to focus on the positives (got that new coffee maker working great!) but rn everything feels kinda blurry.
My hands are shaking so bad I can barely type. They said it's leukemia and everything just went dark.  I don't know what to do anymore. How could this be happening?

My brain feels like it's melting.  They said there's nothing more they can do. I just don't know how to face this, I keep seeing my kids faces and thinking about them without me.  Please, someone tell me what to do.
The doctor called. It's back. I don't know what to do. My whole world is falling apart.
The doctor said it's spread.  How could this be happening? I can't even breathe. What are we going to do?
The thought of my child going through this melanoma diagnosis is simply terrifying.

My brother just hit one year in remission from lymphoma, which is amazing! We're all so relieved. Still, I sometimes get this little pang wondering if it will come back, even though the doctors say everything looks good. It helps to talk about it, though. Anyone else ever feel like that?
rn  cant stop thinking about all the what ifs. did we miss something? should we have done more tests? Stupid spilled coffee on the counter this morning - just another thing going wrong.

Been in remission for 6 months now,  which is great, but rn I just can't shake this feeling something's gonna go wrong. My dog's been super cuddly lately - maybe he senses it too?  Anyway, anyone else deal with this kinda thing after treatment?
My mind races,  unable to grasp this lymphoma diagnosis; I can't even eat.

My chest feels like it's going to explode. I thought being in remission meant I could breathe again, but all I feel is this suffocating dread that it'll come back. How can anyone possibly live like this, always waiting for the other shoe to drop?
Cant stop shaking rn, feels like my head is gonna explode thinking about this stuff and also did I leave the oven on?

Mom's been sleeping a lot lately. I know it's just getting harder rn, and everyone keeps telling me I'm doing great taking care of everything. Still can't help but wonder what we're gonna do without her.  Forgot to buy milk today.
Hey doc, just wanted to let you know I'm feeling a bit bummed about missing out on my niece's graduation. Makes me wish things were different, you know?  How are we looking for next week's treatment?
Missing dad rn, wish he was here to see my new shoes 😔 they're purple tho, his fave 😜

It's been three months since Mom passed.  Every morning I wake up and for a second, everything feels normal. Then it hits me - she's really gone. U think u get used to it but the ache is still there. My car needs an oil change, gotta remember that.  Just feel so lost rn.

It's been six months since Mom passed away from ovarian cancer, and some days I still can't believe she's gone. It feels like just yesterday we were laughing together in the kitchen. Now there's this emptiness that follows me everywhere. I try to be strong for Dad, but sometimes I just want to curl up and cry.
Remission feels empty, like I won a prize no one else understands.

It's weird thinking Mr. Henderson isn't going to be mowing his lawn anymore. I always saw him out there when we were coming home from school. My mom says leukemia is really tough to beat, and she's right. It makes you wonder about a lot of things.  Anyway, I guess it's just different now.

I've been thinking about you a lot lately. Losing him was so sudden and unexpected. It feels surreal, like I'm just waiting for him to walk through the door. I know it's hard right now. Please don't hesitate to reach out if there's anything you need.
Been thinking abt him a lot lately rn. Everything just feels so weird without him around. Forgot to buy milk again - gotta remember that stuff. 😔
It's been six months since I lost him, and some days are easier than others.  I still can't believe he's gone. I keep thinking I'll see his truck pull up the driveway.
Mom's nurse said things are getting harder rn.  I just keep thinking about all the good times we had. Wish I could fix it, but forgot to pick up milk  :(
This chemo is really dragging me down.
still can't believe he's gone.  Pancreatic cancer is so awful rn.  forgot to pick up milk gotta go  :(

It's been incredibly hard watching Dad go through this.  He's always been so strong and independent, but now he needs so much help with even basic tasks. The doctors are doing everything they can, but it feels like we're just trying to slow things down, not make them better. I wish there was more we could do.  I try to stay positive for him, but some days it's a real struggle.
Mom's smile used to be sunshine, now it just feels blurry.
I keep thinking about all the things I won't get to do.
His absence leaves such a gaping hole in our lives.
The house feels so empty without him.

Gotta run to the store, forgot milk again.  Saw the oncologist today.  Melanoma's back. Just feels like one punch after another rn. Guess we'll figure it out. My kid deserves so much better than this.

It's been 3 months since Dad passed away and rn it feels like everything is just so quiet. I miss his laugh, even when he told bad jokes about work. My history test is next week and I can barely focus, ugh.  Forgot my gym bag today too 😩.
rn thinking about him all day. still can't believe he's gone. forgot to buy milk again  😩

Ugh rn everything just feels so heavy.  Missing her like crazy, it's been so quiet around here. Forgot to pick up milk again today, guess I just don't have my head in the game anymore. Can't believe it's already been a month. 💔

Ugh, still can't believe it. Everything happened so fast, one minute we were joking at work, next he was gone. Trying to focus on deadlines but all I can think about is what his family is going through. It just feels so unfair. How do you even begin to process something like this?
This stupid cancer just won't leave me alone! 😠

rn this can't be happening AGAIN!!! after everything we did?? what was the POINT of all that chemo and radiation if it just comes back?!?!  I can't even sleep. forgot to buy milk again.  thx universe, for NOTHING.
This isn't FAIR! I'm 30 freaking years old and now this?  What kind of twisted joke is life playing on me?!  I can't even think straight right now.
This whole thing is so unfair. Why me? 😠  Can't even focus on anything else right now.
This chemo is brutal, I just want to scream sometimes.
It's just NOT FAIR!  My sweet Mary was always so careful, why did this have to happen? I can't even think straight, what am I supposed to do now?!

The neurologist confirmed it today - a brain tumor.  How can this be happening to me? I've always been careful about my health. It just doesn't seem fair.  What am I supposed to do now? My colleagues are counting on me for the quarterly report, and I have a garden that needs tending. But frankly, all I can think about is the injustice of this diagnosis.
It's just NOT FAIR! Why him? He deserved so much more.
This isn't fair!  He fought so hard, for what?  All that pain, and now he's gone anyway. It should have been me. I should be the one lying there. Why him? What did he even DO to deserve this??
It's just NOT FAIR! Why him?!  We were supposed to have more time. What good is this damn treatment if it can't even slow this thing down?

This is SO freaking unfair! My mom just told me I have breast cancer.  Breast cancer? I'm 16! I should be worrying about college apps and prom, not tumors and chemo. WHY ME??? It just feels like my whole life is crashing down right now. What am I supposed to do?
It's just NOT FAIR! Why does HE get prostate cancer? He eats junk food all the time and never goes to the doctor! I can't even sleep anymore thinking about this stupid chemo stuff.  Everything sucks!
This isn't fair! Why did SHE have to get this? What did she EVER do to deserve it?
This is BULLSHIT! My dad's supposed to be on vacation, not fighting for his life!
Is this some kind of sick joke? I did everything they said, and for what? This isn't fair!  I was supposed to be done with all this.
This can't be happening again. I'm so done!  Why me?! 😠🤬🤯
IT'S NOT FAIR I BEAT THIS THING AND NOW MY LIFE IS RUINED?!?!
It's just NOT FAIR!  Why me? Why now? This can't be happening.

This isn't fair! My child, barely old enough to understand what's happening, now has to face this monstrous disease. How could this possibly be happening to us?  What did we do to deserve this torture?
this is BULLSHIT my kid deserves better than this rn im gonna lose it all i cant even sleep anymore cuz the dryer keeps making that damn noise
WHY? I don't understand! It's just not FAIR!! u got to be kidding me rn, did he REALLY have to go?? I can't even sleep, and my coffee maker broke this morning - of all days.
Why is this always happening to me? I just want it gone!
It's been MONTHS and I still can't sleep.  How could he have done this to us? What did we do to deserve this nightmare?
Stupid chemo  took her too fast. rn forgot to get milk again 🤬😩
This is BULLSHIT!! U think I can just FIGHT this AGAIN?? Rn all I want is pizza.

It's just NOT FAIR! I did EVERYTHING right - ate healthy, exercised, never smoked. And this is what I get?! Stuck with tubes and pills, watching my life fade away while everyone else goes on like nothing happened. Why me? What did I DO to deserve this??  I can barely even get out of bed anymore.
How is this fair? My kid deserves a chance at a normal life.

It's been FIVE YEARS and I still get these stabbing pains! What was the POINT of all that chemo if I'm going to be stuck like this forever?  I can barely hold a cup of coffee without it throbbing.  Is this REALLY my life now??

This isn't fair! We did everything right, followed every rule.  All those months of chemo, the surgery, the prayers... for what? It's back already? I can't breathe, I feel like screaming. How are we supposed to go through this AGAIN?  What is the point anymore?
Seriously? It's back again? I thought we were done with this melanoma crap.  How are we supposed to deal with this a second time? This just isn't fair. What are we even going to do now?  Ugh.
This is freaking impossible, it's coming back AGAIN after all that?!

They said they'd caught it early, that I was lucky! LUCKY? My own daughter is gone because of this, and now they say they caught it early?! I don't even want to hear it anymore. I can barely sleep, everything reminds me of her. How could they be so callous, promising "early detection" when it doesn't mean a damn thing in the end?
This isn't fair, man! You're way too young for this crap. I don't get it. What are we supposed to do now? 😠
seriously? rn gotta figure out who's gonna water the geraniums  thx Dad for giving me this crap to deal with
WHY ME?!?! this is SO unfair rn, i can't even SLEEP and my stupid dog won't stop barking!  🤬
Hey! I was thinking about you and wanted to say treatments can be tough but they're helping so many people. You got this.  Let me know if there's anything I can bring over.

I can't believe this is happening again. My brain keeps circling back to that first time, and all the uncertainty I felt. Still, Dr. Evans seemed really positive today, so maybe this time will be different. Maybe we can beat this thing for good.
It's terrifying, but they said this new treatment might work, and we have to believe in something.
Got the news today.  Really scary stuff. Trying to stay positive though - gotta keep fighting for me and my family.
My head is spinning.  Pancreatic cancer? How can this be happening? I'm only 28. I have to fight this. There has to be a way.  Please tell me there's a way.
It's a setback, for sure, but Dr. Lee seems confident about the new chemo plan. We're going to fight this thing, just like before.

It's been encouraging to hear about your remission. While there's always a bit of anxiety with these things, I genuinely believe you've got this. Focusing on what lies ahead and the positive changes in your health will serve you well. I'm here for you every step of the way.

Hey neighbor,  just wanted to check in rn. Heard about your prostate and figured I'd reach out. Thinking of you - hoping things get easier soon. Got extra tomatoes from my garden if u want some? Let me know!

rn hubby's been in remission for almost 6 months now, so fingers crossed that continues! Still nervous about checkups but trying to keep busy with the garden - those tomatoes are coming along nicely.  thx
Thinking of you both and sending all my love; we'll find a way through this.

The doctor said "stage 1" which is good news I guess, but lung cancer? Me? It's all just so surreal right now, like trying to wrap my head around something impossible. Still, gotta focus on the positive, get this treatment started and kick this thing to the curb.

Just got diagnosed with colon cancer.  This isn't what I expected at 25. Feeling really scared and unsure about everything. But I'm going to fight this. It won't be easy, but I'll figure it out, one step at a time.  Wish me luck.

The melanoma diagnosis was a shock, and I'm definitely worried about what's ahead. Still, my doctor seems optimistic about treatment options, and I'm determined to fight this thing head-on.  I have to stay strong for my family.

Ugh, they said it came back.  Honestly, I thought we had this beat last time. But I'm remembering what you were like through all that before – so strong! We can fight this again, together. It might be tough for a while but remember how far we've come already?

Just got diagnosed with breast cancer and I'm honestly kind of freaking out. It all happened so fast, and it feels like my life just flipped upside down.  But there are so many amazing stories out there about people beating this thing, and that gives me some strength to keep going. I know it's going to be a long road, but I have to believe things will get better.

Hey man, I was thinking about you. It's tough to see you going through this leukemia thing, and honestly, it makes me scared sometimes.  But I know how strong you are, and I truly believe we can find ways to make things better. We gotta keep fighting for every good day we get.  Let me know if there's anything at all I can do.
The doctor said it's come back, but they have a new treatment plan.  I'm trying to stay strong and believe this can work. It's scary, but I know we have to keep fighting.

I've been going through radiation for this brain tumor, and some days are tougher than others. I try to keep my chin up, though. My oncologist says there's been some good progress, so maybe, just maybe, things will get better from here. Still scary, of course, but I'm clinging to that bit of good news.
While I'm not sure what the future holds, I'm still able to enjoy my morning coffee and read the news.  It helps me focus on the positive aspects of each day.
Looking forward to staying in remission and living a full life.

Got the news today. Brain tumor.  It's definitely a curveball. Still, I know there are amazing doctors out there, and treatments keep getting better all the time. Going to tackle this head-on.  Let me know if you guys want to grab coffee sometime soon, need a distraction.
Mom always believed in fighting for what she wanted, and I think that's what she would want us to do now.  Remembering her strength helps me focus on finding positive ways to honor her memory.

Seriously rn u guys I just wanna be normal again. Ugh my head hurts so bad.  This lymphoma is really making everything hard but im still gonna try and beat it. Even gotta finish that stupid history paper.
Fingers crossed chemo keeps shrinking those tumors like last time.
This news is hard to swallow, but I need to figure out my next steps and fight for every day.

Some days are harder than others. I keep replaying everything in my head, wondering what I could have done differently.  But then I remember the doctor saying this lymphoma is treatable, and I hold onto that. I just need to keep fighting.

The scan results weren't what I wanted to see, and honestly, it's been hard to wrap my head around it all.  But they caught this thing early last time, so we can fight it again. I just have to keep going.
It's tough sometimes, wondering if this will work.  But Dr. S seems really confident and I know he knows his stuff. We can do this.

Hey neighbor, just checking in. Heard about your prostate situation and wanted to say I'm thinking of you. My dad had some health stuff a while back and they found this new treatment that really helped him. It's tough, I know, but stay strong! Sending positive vibes your way.

Ugh, so they said it came back. Kinda bummed but we knew this was a possibility, right? It's just a detour on our road trip,  not the end. Still gonna beat this thing. Oh and btw, did u hear that new Taylor Swift song? Total bop! 😜

It's tough to hear about Dad's diagnosis. I know this is a lot to process right now.  Still, I'm confident his medical team will do everything they can to manage things going forward. We'll be there every step of the way, supporting him and focusing on making the best of each day.

Mom's diagnosis is certainly a shock, but I am determined to do everything I can to help her through this. We will find the best doctors and explore all available treatment options.  She has always been so strong and resilient, and I know she will face this challenge with courage. There are many advancements in brain cancer treatment these days, so we need to stay positive and focus on finding a path forward.

Hey, just wanted to say I've been thinking about u rn.  It's gotta be tough dealing with all this stuff, and I know there are going to be hard days. Still, my dad says they've made some incredible progress lately with colon cancer treatments, so maybe they can find something that works for you too. Oh, and by the way, did you see that new Spiderman movie?
Fingers crossed for good news at my next scan!
The news of my lymphoma returning is certainly difficult to process. There are dark moments, I won't lie, but I'm determined to fight this again. I have to believe there's still a path forward.

The oncologist said there are new treatment options for pancreatic cancer, and I'm scheduled to meet with a specialist next week. It's a bit overwhelming to think about, but I'm determined to explore all my choices and do what's best for me moving forward.  I trust the medical team and feel positive about this next step.

The doctor confirmed it today: John's colon cancer has returned. We knew there was always a chance it could come back, but facing this again brings a heavy weight. Still, John is strong and we will fight this together.  We found the best oncologist and I trust in his expertise. This road won't be easy, but we have to believe that John can beat this once more.

The doctor confirmed it's back today, and while my heart feels like it's in my throat, I know we have to fight this again.  We'll find the best doctors, get him enrolled in every trial available, and do everything we can to give him more time.
Back to school shopping today - little man's outgrown all his clothes. It's crazy how much they change even after remission. Fingers crossed everything stays this good.

Ugh, Mrs. Peterson's garden is looking amazing this year, all those roses... Anyway rn I just keep thinking about how my mom always said she'd be around to see me graduate. I know it probably won't happen, but part of me still wishes she could beat this. She's such a fighter.  I want her to get better so bad. Maybe there's still some kind of miracle out there.

It's been a rough couple of weeks since Dad started chemo, seeing him so tired and sick is heartbreaking. But I keep remembering what the doctor said, and I know he's got this. We're all behind him fighting for his recovery.
Despite this setback, I know you have the strength to face it head-on.

The fatigue is really starting to get to me, it's harder and harder to focus on anything for long.  Still, I can't help but think about that scan coming up next week.  Maybe this time we'll see some real progress. The doctors are optimistic, and their belief gives me strength too. If I just keep pushing forward, there's a chance for things to get better.

The oncologist confirmed the recurrence today. It feels like we're back at square one, facing down this monster again. Still, I know there are new treatments out there, and I have to believe they can work for him.
My little girl just got diagnosed with ovarian cancer. I keep replaying it in my head - how can this be happening? Still, we have to fight. We'll find a doctor who will help her beat this.

This whole chemo thing is really getting to me rn.  My head's foggy, I keep forgetting stuff, like did I feed the cat already?  But Dr. Smith said it might shrink the tumor some and that gives me something to hold onto.
My sweet girl got diagnosed today. It's breast cancer, and I can't even wrap my head around it. We'll fight this, though. She's strong.

The oncologist called today. The lung cancer is back, and it's spread. I must admit, a cold wave of fear washed over me. My mind immediately went to those dark days before treatment. But then, I thought about how far I’ve come, the strength I found within myself. This time will be different. This time, I am ready to fight again.

The chemo stopped working and they're saying it's spread again. I just don't know how much more of this I can take. My mind is racing, I can't sleep, and all I see is the darkness closing in.  But then I think about my grandkids, their laughter, their sweet faces...and I have to keep fighting. For them.

My head is spinning. I can't believe this is happening to me. Brain tumor? It feels so surreal. But you know what? They said they caught it early and there are treatments.  Treatments that work.  I have to keep fighting, right? There has to be a way through this.
Mom's been diagnosed with pancreatic cancer, and it feels like a ton of bricks just landed on me - but we're gonna fight this together.

Mom passed away last month from lung cancer. It's been so hard, but I keep thinking about all the times she told me to be strong and that everything would work out.  Maybe someday things will feel a little brighter again.
God, please let this chemo work, I can't breathe without it anymore.

Just got diagnosed with melanoma. It's scary stuff, I won't lie. Been feeling pretty overwhelmed trying to wrap my head around all of this. But talking to everyone here and hearing about your journeys gives me a sense that there is light at the end of this tunnel.  I know I have a tough road ahead but I'm determined to fight this thing.
Remission feels good, even if I'm cautious about celebrating too soon.

The doctors talked about comfort and keeping things manageable. It's hard to focus sometimes, but I keep thinking about all those times we laughed together on the porch swing. I really want more of those moments.
Dad's numbers are up again. This is harder than I thought it would be, but we gotta keep looking for options, there has to be something out there.

John's been sleeping so much lately, and it breaks my heart to see him like this. The doctor said it's normal with where he's at, but I can't help wishing for something more.  Maybe a miracle, maybe just a little bit of extra time. It feels impossible some days, you know? But when John opens his eyes and smiles, even faintly, I cling to that feeling. That's what keeps me going.

It's hard to shake this feeling of uncertainty.  The chemo is rough, but I know it's necessary.  Sometimes I get so tired, both physically and mentally, that I just want to give up. But then I think about my grandkids, about seeing them grow up. That keeps me going. I have to believe this will work out.
Hey, been thinking of you.  It's rough, but I have faith you'll find ways to make this next chapter as good as possible.  Let me know if you need anything at all.
The doctor thinks we can tackle this, and I'm ready to give it my all.

I keep thinking about those clinical trial pamphlets.  The doctors say palliative care is for comfort now, but there are so many trials open. I wish they'd let me try one more thing, even if it's just a slim chance.   Maybe something could buy us some time.

Hey man, chemo is rough this week. Brain's all foggy, can barely focus on work. Still, gotta keep pushing through, you know? Thinking about how good it'll be to finally kick this leukemia's ass.  Gotta believe in that brighter future.

They said it's a brain tumor. I keep thinking about him, all alone in that hospital bed. It feels like a nightmare, like I can't wake up from this awful dream.  But we have to fight this, right? We have to find a way through. He's strong. We're strong together.

Ugh, so rn my neighbor got her breast cancer back  😩 It's been 5 years, and I thought she was done with it. My cat is being SO needy today too, like she knows something's wrong. Anyway, I'm trying to be positive for her cuz she always does that for me. She's gotta fight this!  💪💖

Hey man, just checking in.  How's the treatment going? Been thinking about you. It must be tough, but I'm confident you'll kick this thing's butt. Let me know if there's anything I can do to help out.

He's so tired lately. Wish we could get him eating again. They said the chemo wouldn't work anymore but maybe there's something else out there? I just want him to feel better, even if it's just for a little while longer.  Praying for a miracle.

It's been rough losing my neighbor to lung cancer recently. Still, I remember how he always kept pushing forward, even when things were tough. His spirit really inspires me to keep working towards my own goals.
It's encouraging to see you doing so well in remission; I'm cautiously optimistic for the future.
His leukemia is in remission, and I'm cautiously dreaming of a future where we can just be a normal family again.

Hey man, been thinking about you.  Lung stuff is rough, I know. Been there myself. Had some tough days lately, honestly just trying to keep my head above water sometimes. But, doc says I'm doing good and in remission now. Hang in there - it gets better.
Even though it feels impossible now, I have to keep believing things will get better someday.

It's been six months since we lost her, and some days I just can't breathe. The thought of all those missed milestones... birthdays, graduations, maybe even grandkids someday... it's a weight I carry everywhere. But then I see a story about someone beating the odds, a new research breakthrough, anything really. And I cling to that, thinking maybe someday, we'll see something similar for this awful disease. It has to be possible, right?

My mom was diagnosed with ovarian cancer a few months ago, and it's been really tough. They say it's advanced, but we're still doing everything we can.  It's hard to stay positive sometimes, wondering what the future holds, but I keep remembering how strong she is. I truly believe she can make it through this, even if it's a long road.

Hi [neighbor's name]. Thinking of you and wanted to check in. It's amazing that you're doing so well after everything. I know it hasn't been easy, but your strength is inspiring. Wishing you continued good health moving forward.  Let me know if there's anything you need.

Focusing on my treatment plan today was harder than I expected. My mind keeps wandering to what comes next, and honestly, it's scary. Still, Dr. Lee mentioned positive results in trials for this type of tumor, so I'm holding onto that.  Even if the road ahead is tough, I have to believe there's a chance for a better outcome.

They said you're in remission now. That's amazing, right? But I keep thinking... maybe if I had pushed harder for you to see a doctor sooner, things wouldn't have gotten so bad.  I know it's not rational, but I can't shake this feeling that I messed something up.

His colon cancer has been so aggressive. I should have insisted he go to the doctor sooner. Maybe if I'd pushed harder, he wouldn't be fading away like this. What kind of wife am I to let him suffer? I can barely function, constantly thinking about what I could have done differently. The weight of it all is crushing me.

It's back. The doctor said it's spread. I knew I should have made her eat healthier, pushed her to exercise more. Maybe if I had done things differently... This is all my fault. How could I let this happen?
Is this my fault? I always pushed myself so hard.
This wouldn't have happened if I'd just listened to my doctor sooner.
He hates hospital food so much. I wish I'd pushed for him to eat healthier all those years ago.

I can't stop thinking maybe if I'd pushed harder for him to see another doctor sooner... none of this would have happened. What was I even thinking? Everything feels so pointless now. I just want it all to be undone.  How could I let him down like that?
I suppose I should have pushed for more screenings years ago. Now it feels like this prostate cancer diagnosis is all my fault. My mind just races trying to figure out how things could be different.
Is this leukemia all my fault? Maybe I shouldn't have worked so much when they were younger.  I just wish I could go back and do things differently.
It's all my fault. I should have caught it sooner, insisted on more tests, pushed harder. Now she's gone and I just can't breathe, I can't function.  What am I supposed to do now?
Guess I should've made him eat more broccoli.
It's all my fault, she wouldn't have gotten sick if I hadn't...  I can't even finish that thought.

It's all my fault. I should have pushed her to get checked sooner. If only I hadn't brushed off those early symptoms, maybe things would be different. Now she's gone, and this emptiness is unbearable. How could I let her down like this? I keep replaying every moment, searching for where I went wrong.  I can't escape the feeling that I failed her in the worst possible way.

I just don't understand why this happened to her. We were supposed to go to college together, plan our futures... what did I do wrong? Maybe if I had been there more for her, made her laugh more, it wouldn't be like this. It feels like my fault somehow.  How could this happen to someone so amazing? I can barely breathe.

Ugh rn i cant stop thinking it's my fault. My dad smokes but like, he always said he'd quit when I was older and I guess I just nagged him too little. Didnt want to be annoying. Now he's got lung cancer. Kinda lost track of what time it is lol
I keep thinking maybe if I had noticed sooner...  He seemed so tired all the time but I just thought it was old age.  Now he's gone and I feel like it's my fault.
They said it was gone. Gone! I did everything right. Healthy food, no smoking, even moved us away from the factory.  And now this. It's all my fault. My poor baby. What have I done?

It's all my fault. I should have made him go to the doctor sooner. Maybe if I nagged more, maybe if I hadn't let him brush it off... now he's gone and I can't stop thinking about what I could have done differently. I just want him back.  Everything feels broken.
Perhaps I could have done more to convince him to see a doctor sooner.

Why didn't I push harder for him to see another doctor?  We wasted so much time. Maybe if I hadn't been such a pain about that stupid internship this summer, he could've caught it sooner. God, this is all my fault. Can't even focus on work rn, keep seeing his face everywhere. Forgot to pay the internet bill too, great.

Ugh, rn I'm just feeling kinda bummed that maybe I pushed him to play outside too much last summer.  He loved it tho, always running around. Silly me forgot to pack his favorite snack today for chemo.

The doctor confirmed it today. Prostate cancer. I knew there were issues, but part of me thought maybe it wouldn't be this serious.  Now I can't help but wonder if I ignored early warning signs, if I could have done something differently to prevent this. It feels like a terrible burden to carry.
Hey, I was just thinking... remember when you used to say you wanted to take that trip to see the grandkids? It makes me feel bad that we never got around to it.  Let's talk soon!

It's been a year since his last treatment and I'm so relieved he's in remission.  But I can't help but wonder if maybe, somehow, this is all my fault. Did I miss something? Could I have done more to catch it earlier?
Shoulda made him eat more veggies when he was little rn i feel like it's all my fault and i can't even concentrate on this stupid recipe forgot the garlic again  damn it.
This is all my fault, rn I cant even think straight, spilled coffee everywhere, colon cancer's back.
Was there something I could have done differently?

It's all my fault. If I hadn't convinced her to try that new juice cleanse maybe she wouldn't be gone now. What was I thinking? It's just me and this empty apartment,  everything smells like her perfume and I can't breathe.
Did I do something to make him get sick again?
I keep going back and forth. I know he made all the right choices with his treatment, but I can't help thinking maybe there was something else we could have done. Should we have caught it earlier?
I keep replaying all those doctor's appointments in my head. Did I miss something? Should I have pushed harder for more tests?  It just feels like I could have done more.

I can't stop thinking about all the what ifs. What if I'd pushed him to get checked sooner? Maybe if I hadn't been so focused on work, we could have caught it earlier. Now he's gone and it's all my fault. How am I supposed to live with this?  I feel like I'm drowning.
If only I'd insisted on those colonoscopies sooner...

It's back. The doctor said it's everywhere now. I should have listened to you about the supplements. Maybe if I had pushed harder for those blood tests last year... this wouldn't be happening. I just feel so helpless and like it's all my fault.  I can barely breathe.

It's all my fault. I should have made him eat more vegetables, taken better care of his health.  Now he's just wasting away. What if this is punishment for something I did wrong? I can't sleep, I can't eat... what was the point of anything?
Wish I'd pushed him to see the doctor sooner.  It's back, u know? And rn it just feels like my fault somehow.  Left the laundry in the dryer again - gotta go get that out before it wrinkles.

It's my fault.  I should have made him go to the doctor sooner. Maybe then this wouldn't be happening. How could I be so blind? I just wanted him to be happy. Now he's slipping away and it's all because of me.

He wouldn't eat anything I made anymore. Said it all tasted like ash. Then he started losing weight, even though I tried everything. Was it the way I cooked it? Maybe if I’d pushed him to see the doctor sooner, maybe if I hadn't… God, what am I supposed to do now?  It's all my fault.
Maybe this is my fault for not catching it sooner.

It's my fault. I should have made him see a doctor sooner. Maybe if I hadn't nagged him about eating so much red meat, he wouldn't be in this mess now. What kind of husband am I? All these years and I didn't even notice something was wrong. My stomach is in knots, I can barely breathe. How could I let this happen?
Wish i'd made him eat more veggies rn...forgot to buy carrots at the store today 😥
My chemo's making me so tired. I keep thinking maybe if I'd gone to the doctor sooner, this wouldn't be happening.

I just can't stop thinking it's my fault. I should have made him see a doctor sooner. Maybe if I hadn't been so insistent on waiting, this wouldn't be happening. Now he's going through all this and I feel completely helpless. What if I ruined everything?

I keep replaying everything in my head. Did I miss something? Should I have pushed harder for more tests?  Maybe if we'd caught it sooner... God, this is all my fault. I can barely breathe. What am I supposed to do now?

His doctor said he's in remission now, which is amazing news, truly. But I can't shake this feeling that maybe if I'd pushed him to go to the doctor sooner...maybe things wouldn't have gotten so bad. It's just hard not to think about what I could have done differently.

His blood counts were so low again today. I keep replaying every decision we made – about treatment, about moving closer to the hospital, even what we ate for dinner last week. Was there something else we could have done? Did I push him too hard? Maybe if I had been more supportive, things would be different.  It's a heavy weight to carry.
Should have visited more, I feel like it's my fault now.

The doctor confirmed it's colon cancer. Stage II, they said, which is supposed to be good news compared to what else it could have been. Still, I keep replaying every decision in my head. Did I push Dad hard enough to see the doctor sooner? Should I have noticed something was wrong? Maybe if I had been more attentive, this wouldn't be happening.  I just feel so responsible.
If only I had insisted on him getting checked sooner, maybe things would be different.

Hey dude, congrats on hitting remission! I'm so happy for you. It's crazy to think about everything you've been through, makes me feel kinda bad that I was stressing over stupid stuff like exams while you were dealing with all that.  Anyways, let's hang out soon and celebrate!

I keep thinking it was my fault rn.  Maybe if I'd pushed him to go to the doctor sooner... he's gone and I just feel sick all the time. Did I do enough? Can't sleep. Forgot to feed the dog this morning.

Mom's been so tired lately, barely eats anymore rn. Did I push her too hard? Shoulda just listened when she said she was done with chemo... Stupid lasagna for dinner again, forgot we ran out of parmesan last week.
His colon cancer came back again.  What did I do wrong? Maybe I shouldn't have given him that extra cookie last month. Everything feels like my fault now. We just started to feel hopeful again.

I can't stop thinking about all those times I should have been there for her more, pushed her to get checked sooner.  Was it something I said? Did I miss something obvious? It's my fault she's gone.  How could I have let this happen?
It's all my fault you got sick. I should have done more to protect you. What if it comes back?  I can't live through that again.
Been in remission for 6 months now. Still hard not to think it was something I could've prevented somehow. Fingers crossed it stays this way.

I keep replaying everything in my head. Did I push him to eat healthier? Should I have noticed something was wrong sooner? Maybe if we caught it earlier…  It's just so hard knowing there's nothing more we can do now except make him comfortable.  The doctor said palliative care, but all I hear is "giving up".

Dude, I can't stop thinking about everything we could have done differently.  Maybe if we'd caught it sooner...or maybe if I had been more insistent.  I feel like I should have known something was wrong. It all just feels so unfair.  How could this happen to him?

I'm trying to be supportive, but I can't shake this feeling that I should have done more.  He's always been so active and healthy, maybe if I'd pushed him to see a doctor sooner... It's hard not to think it's my fault somehow, even though everyone says prostate cancer is just bad luck. I just keep replaying everything in my head, wondering what I could have done differently.
It's all my fault. I should have made her see a doctor sooner, maybe then this wouldn't have happened. What kind of spouse lets their wife go through this?

Did I do something wrong?  John's brain tumor was so aggressive... rn I keep thinking maybe if I'd pushed for more scans sooner, he wouldn't have gotten worse so quickly. Stupid, right? He was tired all the time, but it was just stress, I thought. Now the house smells like lilies and everyone keeps saying how strong I am but honestly my stomach hurts constantly. Did I miss something?  Forgot to pick up milk today.

It's just me. Alone again, staring at these pills. What did I do to deserve this?  Every time Michael coughs I imagine it's him starting to go down the same awful path. My fault. It has to be my fault. Maybe if I'd been a better wife, made healthier meals, pushed for checkups sooner... he wouldn't be facing this now. He deserves so much better.  I just keep thinking, what if I had done something different?

This whole thing is my fault, I knew I should have pushed harder for those extra tests years ago. My PSA was a little elevated then, but everything else looked fine. Now it's back and it's worse. Maybe if I hadn't let things slide...I just don't know what to do anymore.
If only I'd insisted on sunscreen more often when she was little...
It's back, and I just feel like I let everyone down again.
Wish I'd called you sooner; maybe things would be different now.
Hey, just checking in.  Did he get his meds today? Rn it feels like everything is my fault for even suggesting this treatment... I'm so tired, forgot to pick up milk again 😩
His remission feels like a gift I don't deserve after how I treated him before he got sick.

I keep going back and forth in my mind. Did I do enough to prevent this? Maybe if we'd caught it sooner... The doctor says it's treatable but it's just so hard. I feel like it should have been me who got sick, not him.  He deserves better than all of this.
It's been quiet since the last scan and I can breathe again, but then this stupid thought keeps popping up – did I do something to make him sick?  I should have done better.

It's all my fault. If I hadn't pushed him to eat so much red meat, maybe this wouldn't be happening. Now he's stuck with chemo and who knows what else.  I can barely sleep, the thought of losing him just eats me up inside. What kind of friend am I?
If only I'd insisted on those mammograms sooner.

The oncologist confirmed what I'd feared: the colon cancer is back. I can't help but wonder if I didn't do enough during my last remission. Perhaps there were dietary changes I should have made, exercises I could have done more diligently.  Focus feels impossible today.

I'm so happy for you that you're in remission, truly! But I can't help but feel like maybe if I had been there more for you, pushed you to get checked sooner... things might have been different.  Maybe it wouldn't have gotten to that stage.

Hey everyone, just wanted to update you all - my PSA levels are finally back in the normal range and rn I'm officially in remission! Been keeping busy trying out new recipes - anyone have any good lasagna ideas?  It feels weird though, like maybe I did something wrong to deserve this whole thing. Still glad it's over. Thx for all the support.

Is this really happening to me? What did I do wrong? Was there something I could have changed, some diet I should have followed, some toxin I should have avoided? This diagnosis feels like punishment for some sin I must have committed in the past. All these years, and now this is how it ends?  I can't even bring myself to tell my family. They deserve better than this burden.

I keep thinking about all the things I could have done differently before Mom's diagnosis. Maybe if I'd been home more, encouraged her to get checkups sooner, something... anything...  could have changed this.

Been helping Mary with some yard work rn, she's doing alright considering. Just wish I'd been there for her more before... maybe I could have caught it sooner.  The zucchini are looking amazing though, u should stop by soon!
His remission is great news, don't get me wrong. But did I push him too hard? Was there something I should have done differently when he was going through treatment?

Mr. Henderson said it's good news that they caught it early and I'm in remission now. But sometimes I still think about all those times I nagged him to go see a doctor. If I hadn't been so annoying maybe he wouldn't have had to deal with this whole thing.  What if I pushed too hard and made it worse somehow?
Mom said she'd still be here if I hadn't been so selfish rn  forgot to buy milk again 😭
Maybe this is all my fault, I shouldn't have made him eat that red meat so often.

I keep replaying our last conversation in my head. Did I push him too hard to eat healthier? Maybe if we'd caught this sooner...  I just feel so helpless right now, like everything is my fault somehow.  He needs me to be strong, but all I can think about are the what-ifs.
It's great news you're in remission! I just can't help but wonder if maybe things would have been different if I had pushed for more tests sooner.

I can't stop thinking it's my fault. Maybe if I'd noticed something sooner, pushed for more tests, done anything differently... now he just sleeps all day.  He barely recognizes me anymore. What was the point of all those years together? All the planning for retirement? It's like everything is falling apart and I can't even hold it together enough to help him.

rn its been 3 weeks since she passed and i cant stop thinking it was my fault.  Shouldnt have let her eat those blueberries that day... maybe if id made her something else it wouldve been different. I havent slept in days. My boss keeps calling about work but i cant even focus. The cat just threw up on the rug - guess thats how things are now.
It's back, and I can't help but think maybe if I had done things differently...

It's all my fault. If I had just been there more, listened better, maybe things would be different now. He kept saying he was fine, but I should have pushed harder, known something was wrong.  I can't stop thinking about what I could have done. What if...what if I wasn't such a terrible friend?
Maybe if I'd pushed him to see a doctor sooner this wouldn't be happening.

I keep thinking about all the things I could have done differently. Was there something I missed? Should I have pushed for more tests earlier? Even though he's in remission now, it's hard not to blame myself sometimes.

They say it's for the best, to stop the treatments that are hurting more than they help. But I can't shake this feeling, this awful weight crushing me. Did I do something wrong? Was there something else I could have done, anything at all, to keep this from happening? Every smile, every laugh, every moment we shared feels tainted now, like a cruel reminder of what I failed to protect them from.

is it my fault she's like this rn?? i gave her those pills every day for years, maybe I didn't do it right?? did I make her sicker? should I have made her go to the doctor sooner? the pain meds aren't working anymore and they say there's nothing else. forgot to put the milk in the fridge this morning, can't even focus.

rn it just doesn't feel real, u know? Everything's so blurry, like I'm watching it from far away.  Dad keeps saying we need to plan things, but for what? Forgot to pick up milk today lol.

They said he was gone? No way. He was just sleeping, right?  Deep sleep, like he used to get after chemo. I keep expecting him to open his eyes any minute. This can't be real. I can't even breathe. It's all a nightmare.
Nah, they're probably wrong, gotta be something else.

My sister's been through so much with this breast cancer thing rn, and it just doesn't seem real. I know she needs treatment but part of me keeps thinking maybe it's all a mistake, you know?  The dr said everything is caught early which is good, right? Anyway, forgot to buy milk today - gotta add that to the list!

My head is spinning. Ovarian cancer? Me? It just can't be right. There has to be some mistake. They must have mixed up my file with someone else's. I feel like I'm going crazy, everything is a blur.  How am I supposed to even deal with this?

Yeah, chemo's starting soon. I guess they said it's the best course of action for this kind of thing. Still feels weird though, like none of this is actually happening. Got to get ready for work later.
Must be a mistake; I'm feeling fine.

This just can't be right.  There must be some mistake. We were so careful about his health, always making sure he got checked regularly. It feels impossible that this is happening. I need to go back and look at the paperwork again. Maybe there's something they missed.

This lymphoma diagnosis just can't be real. I feel like I'm going crazy! The doctors are talking about chemo and all these scary treatments, but it feels like a bad dream.  Someone please tell me this isn't happening. How can I possibly face this? I just want to go back to how things were before.
It can't be real. Mom was always so healthy, she went for walks every day.  This just feels like a bad dream, I keep hoping I'll wake up.

It just doesn't feel real, you know?  Like, they said it's pancreatic cancer but I keep thinking it's gotta be some mistake. Maybe they mixed up my files? It's just too much to wrap my head around right now.

Hey, so they finally got back to me with the results.  It's pancreatic cancer. I just...wow. I mean it can't be *that* bad, right? Maybe there's been some mistake. Gotta stay positive. They said we'll talk about next steps soon.
This can't be real. They must have mixed up my results. There's no way this is happening to me. I feel like I'm in a nightmare and I just want to wake up. Please tell me there's some mistake.
rn got diagnosed with ovarian stuff today... weird, feels like it can't be happening to me tbh. Did anyone bring snacks to the last meeting?  Was craving those mini pretzels.
Dude, they just told me. BRAIN TUMOR?!?!  This can't be real rn. I have so much to do, my plants are dying, and now this?!? It has to be a mistake.

The doctor said it's prostate cancer. Prostate cancer! Me?  It just can't be real. This is a nightmare I need to wake up from. I keep hearing his words over and over, but they sound like they're coming from another room, muffled and distant. How could this happen to me? I felt fine yesterday. I have so much I still want to do.  It's just not fair.
It can't be right; they must have mixed up my results.
They said it's gone! Gone!  Like it was never even there. But I can still feel it, you know? It's right here. This can't be real.  This isn't happening.

Hey, been thinking about everything lately. Kinda weird to be in remission, you know? Still have checkups coming up, but it's good to know things are looking okay for now. I guess life just takes unexpected turns.
We knew this might happen someday, so we're just taking it one step at a time.
Palliative care will help me focus on what matters most now.
They confirmed it's colon cancer. It's a lot to take in, but I guess we just figure out next steps and go from there.

Hey, u know how rn they're just focusing on keeping me comfy? Kinda sucks, but guess it makes sense given everything. My dog keeps me going tho - she's a real cuddle monster! Gotta pick up more treats later. 🐶
Ugh, chemo brain is real rn.  Forgot where I put my keys for like 10 mins lol. Guess this is my new normal for awhile 😅  Still gotta make it to that doc appt tho.
The palliative care team seems thoughtful and supportive; I'm glad they are focusing on comfort now.
It's definitely been an adjustment since my diagnosis, but I'm finding ways to fit in with the new normal. Trying to stay active and engaged as much as possible -  gotta keep things moving.
Got the call from the doc today. Colon cancer, stage 2.  Gotta figure out next steps, but feeling strangely calm about it all.
Things are definitely different these days, but I'm trying to keep up with my usual routine as much as possible.  Maybe we can try that new coffee shop this week?
Guess we'll just keep doing what we're doing, huh?

Took Max to the park today - he's so fast now, it's hard to keep up! Rn it's been 6 months since his last treatment. Crazy how things can change. Still gotta be careful tho, those doctors werent kidding about keeping an eye out for anything weird. Think we're doing ok tho.  I ordered a new toaster oven today - the old one finally gave up.
Just picked up some extra groceries - rn thinkin' we should make those enchiladas u love. Chemo kinda wipes me out sometimes, but I gotta say, this whole thing makes ya appreciate the little things.
Work's been kind of weird lately, everyone tiptoeing around me. Guess they don't know what to say. I'm still going in though, same routine. Gotta keep busy, you know?
